title: The Princess and the Pea
summary: Gentle storybook telling of the famous test - soft, regal narration.
prompt: That night, the queen laid a single pea on the bedstead. Over the pea she stacked twenty mattresses, and over the mattresses twenty more eiderdown quilts. In the morning, the princess sat at breakfast with shadows under her eyes. "Oh, terribly," she sighed when asked how she slept. "There was something so hard in my bed I am quite black and blue all over." And so they knew, at last, she was a true princess.
